title: Fix the XSS hole in the comment renderer
description: # Issue #140 - Stored XSS via comments Security report: posting a comment containing executes in every visitor's browser. starter/comments.js injects user-controlled author and text straight into innerHTML. **Acceptance criteria:** - user-controlled values must never be interpolated into innerHTML (or any raw-HTML API) - render the author and text using safe DOM APIs (textContent / createTextNode) or escape them first - the rendered output keeps the same structure: bold author, then the text
optimal_hint: Ideal: build the nodes with document.createElement('strong'), set author/text via textContent or createTextNode, and appendChild. An escapeHtml() helper plus innerHTML is acceptable but second-best. Using DOMPurify is overkill here but not wrong.
